title: Reading a Bar Graph: Favorite Fruits

steps.instruction: A bar graph shows favorite fruits. The categories are: Apples, Bananas, Grapes. The scale goes from 0 to 10.
steps.work: Categories: Apples, Bananas, Grapes. Scale: 0 to 10.
steps.explanation: Bar graphs have categories (what we're comparing) and a scale (the numbers).

steps.instruction: Look at how high each bar reaches.
steps.work: Apples: bar reaches 7. Bananas: bar reaches 4. Grapes: bar reaches 6.
steps.explanation: Line up the top of each bar with the scale to read its value.

steps.instruction: Which fruit is most popular? How many more chose apples than bananas?
steps.work: Most popular: Apples (7). Difference: 7 − 4 = 3 more chose apples.
steps.explanation: Bar graphs make it easy to compare by looking at bar heights.

title: Reading a Picture Graph with a Key

steps.instruction: A picture graph about pets has a key: each paw print = 2 pets.
steps.explanation: The key tells you what each picture is worth. Not always 1!

steps.instruction: Dogs: 4 pictures. Cats: 3 pictures. Fish: 5 pictures.
steps.explanation: Multiply the number of pictures by the value in the key.
Add all the values to find the total across all categories.
